lessons.title: Variables and Data Types
lessons.content: Kotlin has two ways to declare variables: val (read-only, assigned once) and var (mutable). Kotlin infers types automatically, but you can specify them explicitly.

codeSnippets.code:
val x: Int = 5
var y: String = "hello"
y = "world" // OK, var is mutable

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.content: Functions are declared with the fun keyword. Kotlin supports default parameter values, named arguments, and single-expression functions.
